curriculum.subject: Mathematics
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units extracted from the curriculum source using staged structural extraction.
coreObjectives: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.
Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.

topics: Introduction to rational numbers
Representation of rational numbers on the number line
Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
Decimal representation of rational numbers
Introduction to irrational numbers
Proof of irrationality of √2 and √3
The square root spiral

competency_groups.title: Understanding of Numbers and Real Number Set Properties
competency_groups.description: Develops understanding of numbers, including the set of real numbers and its properties.
competencies: Understand the concept of a rational number.
Represent rational numbers on the number line.
Understand the properties of rational numbers.
Explain the concept of density of rational numbers.
Compute decimal representation of rational numbers.
Understand the concept of irrational numbers.
Prove the irrationality.
Construct the square root spiral.

competency_groups.title: Modelling Linear Growth/Decay and Identifying Patterns
competency_groups.description: Models linear growth and decay using linear polynomials; identifies patterns in linear relationships.
competencies: Understand the meaning of an algebraic expression.
Define a polynomial.
Identify the degree, terms and coefficients of terms in a polynomial.
Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.
Explain and identify patterns in linear relationships.
Identify the slope and y-intercept of a linear equation in two variables.

competency_groups.title: Sequences and Progressions: Rules and Applications
competency_groups.description: Identifies patterns in sequences; determines recursive and explicit rules for APs and GPs.
competencies: Understand the concept of a sequence of numbers.
Identify the pattern in a sequence and predict the next few terms.
Determine the recursive and explicit rules for different sequences.
Obtain the terms of sequence given its recursive and explicit rule.
Identify Arithmetic Progressions (AP).
Determine the nth term of an AP.
Visualise an AP graphically.
Identify Geometric Progressions (GP).
Determine the nth term of a GP.
Visualise a GP graphically.

competency_groups.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities and Factorisation
competency_groups.description: Uses computational thinking to visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.
competencies: Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.
Determine the factors of algebraic expressions using identities.
Interpret factors of quadratic expressions through geometric models.
Find simplified versions of rational expressions.

competency_groups.title: Solving Linear Equations and Modelling Contexts
competency_groups.description: Models daily-life phenomena using representations; solves pairs of linear equations graphically and algebraically.
competencies: Understand the concept of a linear equation in two variables.
Graph a pair of linear equations.
Solve a pair of linear equations graphically.
Solve a pair of linear equations through the methods of substitution and elimination.
Determine the nature of solutions of a pair of linear equations.

competency_groups.title: Coordinate Geometry: Location and Distance
competency_groups.description: Specifies locations using coordinates; computes distance between points.
competencies: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.
Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.
Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.
Determine whether three points lie in a straight line using coordinates.

competency_groups.title: Euclid's Axioms and Geometric Constructions
competency_groups.description: Proves mathematical statements using axioms; constructs geometric shapes.
competencies: Describe how geometry grew from the practical needs ancient civilisations.
Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.
State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.
Define parallelism of straight lines.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates

competency_groups.title: Lines and Angles: Theorems and Reasoning
competency_groups.description: Follows proof by contradiction; applies computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas.
competencies: Explain the notion of an angle and a ray.
Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.
State and apply the linear pair theorem and its converse.
Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.

competency_groups.title: Triangle Congruence Theorems
competency_groups.description: Identifies uses of triangle rigidity; proves conditions for congruence.
competencies: Explain that a triangle is rigid.
Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.
Use the SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS congruence conditions.

competency_groups.title: Quadrilaterals: Properties and Symmetry
competency_groups.description: Proves characterisations of parallelograms; understands central symmetry.
competencies: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.
Prove the midpoint theorem.

competency_groups.title: Circle Geometry and Properties
competency_groups.description: Explains relationships between chords, arcs, and angles; constructs circumcircles.
competencies: State the definition of a circle.
Explain why there exists a unique circle through three non-collinear points.
Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle.

competency_groups.title: Area Calculations: Heron's and Brahmagupta's Formulas
competency_groups.description: Visualises area of triangles using Heron's formula; understands generalisation.
competencies: Define perimeter as the length around the boundary of any shape.
Compute the circumference of a circle and the length of an arc.

competency_groups.title: Surface Area and Volume Calculations
competency_groups.description: Computes surface area and volume of solid objects; uses computational thinking to compare shapes.
competencies: Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.
Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.
Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height.

competency_groups.title: Statistical Data Analysis
competency_groups.description: Collects, organises, visualise and interpret data; computes weighted average.
competencies: Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.
Read and interpret stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs.

competency_groups.title: Probability: Empirical and Theoretical Concepts
competency_groups.description: Estimates empirical probability; applies theoretical probability definitions.
competencies: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.
Define theoretical probability of an event.

competency_weightage: Competencies are integrated across all units with emphasis on computational thinking (CG-9), mathematical modelling (C-3.2/C-8.1), and geometric reasoning (C-4.x, C-7.x).
Specific competencies like 'Prove theorems' (C-4.2, C-7.3) and 'Visualise identities' (C-7.2) are assessed through problem-solving tasks.

bloom_levels.level_1: Remembering: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts.
bloom_levels.level_2: Understanding: Demonstrate understanding by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions.

bloom_levels.level_3: Applying: Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge and techniques.

bloom_levels.level_4: Analysing: Examine information into parts, identify motives or causes.
bloom_levels.level_5: Evaluating: Present and defend opinions based on criteria.
bloom_levels.level_6: Creating: Compile information in a new pattern or propose alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.component: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment

practical_assessment.description: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books

prescribed_books: Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools, class IX (CBSE)
Mathematics exemplar problems for class IX (NCERT)

project_assessment.component: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects
project_assessment.description: Integral part of the learning process, moving beyond summative examinations.

outcomes: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.

outcomes: Specify locations and the position of one point relative to another point using coordinates.
Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates.

outcomes: Compute the distance between two points using coordinates.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoint of the line- segment between two points in the 2-D plane
outcomes: Compute the position of the midpoint of a line segment using coordinates.
Find the coordinates of the fourth vertex of a parallelogram given the other three.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: History of geometry
outcomes: Describe how geometry grew from the practical needs ancient civilisations.
Describe contributions of India, Egypt and Greece to the development of geometric ideas.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
outcomes: Explain the construction of a square as given in the Sulbasutras.
Justify simple constructions using the axioms.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Discovering Euclid's definitions
outcomes: Understand the role of definitions, axioms, and postulates.
Explain that there are elements of plane geometry (point, line, surface) for which we have an intuitive sense.

learning_outcomes.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
learning_outcomes.topic: Axioms: Axioms of measurement and rules for geometric objects
outcomes: State the 5 postulates of Euclidean geometry.
Define parallelism of straight lines.

learning_outcomes.topic: Rays and angles
outcomes: Explain the notion of an angle.
Explain the notion of a ray.

learning_outcomes.topic: Measures of angles
outcomes: State that a straight angle equals two right angles and measures 180° while a right angle measures 90°.
Classify angles as acute, right, obtuse, or reflex.

learning_outcomes.topic: Intersecting lines and angles
outcomes: Define parallelism.
State and apply the linear pair theorem and its converse.

learning_outcomes.topic: Pairs of angles
outcomes: Identify corresponding, alternate, and interior angles.
Explain transitivity of parallelism.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems and examples on intersecting lines
outcomes: Follow proof by contradiction in geometry.
Prove that vertically opposite angles are equal.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems and examples on parallel lines
outcomes: Explain why a triangle must have at least two acute angles; why it cannot have two obtuse angles, or all three angles less than 60°.
Apply computational thinking to analyse geometric ideas by breaking constructions into ordered steps.

learning_outcomes.topic: Practical applications of triangles
outcomes: Explain that a triangle is rigid, unlike a quadrilateral.
Identify uses of triangle rigidity.

learning_outcomes.topic: Proofs of conditions of congruence of triangles
outcomes: Describe what it means for two triangles to be congruent.
Identify correspondence between the vertices, sides, and angles of two congruent triangles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theorems on triangles
outcomes: Use the SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS congruence conditions.
Prove the basic properties of isosceles triangles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Propositions and their converse
outcomes: Explain the notion of a proposition.
Identify the converse of a given proposition.

learning_outcomes.topic: Problems based on applications of theorems on triangles
outcomes: Explain that not all converses are true; use counter examples to show that some converses are false.
Justify the role of diagram accuracy.

learning_outcomes.topic: Properties of parallelograms
outcomes: Frame a precise definition of a 4-gon.
Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.

learning_outcomes.topic: Important theorems related to parallelograms and their proof
outcomes: Prove various characterisations of a parallelogram.
Find the coordinates of the midpoint of a line segment given its end points.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoint theorem and its applications
outcomes: Prove the midpoint theorem.
Prove a converse of the midpoint theorem.

learning_outcomes.topic: Understanding the notion of central symmetry in the context of parallelograms
outcomes: Understand reflection and rotation symmetries of 4-gons.
Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

learning_outcomes.topic: Practical applications and uses of circles
outcomes: Explain how circular wheels have influenced transport, farming, building, and technology.
Identify cultural motifs involving circles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Definitions related to a circle — centre, diameter, and radius
outcomes: State the definition of a circle.
Explain the meanings of the terms ‘chord’, ‘diameter’, ‘radius’, ‘arc’, ‘segment’, and ‘sector’.

learning_outcomes.topic: Chords and the angles they subtend
outcomes: Explain why 'equal chords subtend equal angles at the centre'.
Explain why 'chords that subtend equal angles at the centre are equal'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Midpoints and perpendicular bisectors of chords
outcomes: Explain why 'the line from the centre of a circle to the midpoint of a chord is perpendicular to the chord'.
Explain why 'a perpendicular from the centre to a chord bisects the chord'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Distance of chords from the centre
outcomes: State the relationship between length of a chord and its distance from the centre of the circle.
Explain why 'equal chords are equidistant from the centre (and conversely)'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Subtended angles by an arc
outcomes: Explain what 'angle subtended by an arc at the centre' means.
Explain why 'the angle subtended by an arc at the centre is double the angle subtended by the arc at any point on the remaining part of the circle'.
Explain why 'angles in the same segment of a circle are equal'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Cyclicity of points
outcomes: Determine when four given points are concyclic.
Explain why 'a quadrilateral with supplementary opposite angles is cyclic, and conversely'.

learning_outcomes.topic: Perimeter of shapes
outcomes: Define perimeter as the length around the boundary of any shape.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes, apply step-by-step methods to calculate perimeter.

learning_outcomes.topic: Perimeter of a circle: Introduction to Pi and its irrationality
outcomes: Explain that the circumference-to-diameter ratio is constant for all circles.
List historical approximations to π (from Archimedes, Aryabhata, and Zu Chongzhi).

learning_outcomes.topic: Length of an arc
outcomes: Compute the circumference of a circle and the length of an arc.
Apply ideas of circle perimeter and arc-length to real-world contexts.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles
outcomes: Explain why a median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of equal area.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heron's formula
outcomes: Use Heron’s formula to compute the area of a triangle from its sides.
Explain why Heron’s formula is a ‘special case’ of Brahmagupta’s formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Squaring a rectangle: Proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
outcomes: Explain the classical problem of ‘squaring’ a given shape.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of a circle: derivation
outcomes: Explain how ancient civilisations approximated the area of a circle.
Compute the area of a circle using the formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Area of the sector of a circle
outcomes: Explain and use the formula for area of a sector of a circle.
Solve problems on areas of sectors and segments of circles.

learning_outcomes.topic: Brahmagupta's formula for area of a cyclic 4-gon
outcomes: State Brahmagupta’s formula for the area of a cyclic quadrilateral in terms of its sides.
Explain why Heron’s formula is a ‘special case’ of Brahmagupta’s formula.

learning_outcomes.topic: Heron's formula as a special case of Brahmagupta's formula
outcomes: Explain the notion of ‘special case’ and ‘generalisation’ in mathematics.
Use computational thinking to break down shapes, apply step-by-step methods.

learning_outcomes.topic: Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres) and right circular cones
outcomes: Recognise cuboids, cubes, cylinders, cones, pyramids, and spheres in real-life situations.
Compute the surface area and volume of these shapes.

learning_outcomes.topic: Compute the surface area and volume of a cuboid.
outcomes: Explain how a cube is a ‘special case’ of a cuboid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Describe a right circular cylinder using its radius and height.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a cylinder.

learning_outcomes.topic: Recognise cones in daily life, and describe them using radius and height.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a cone.

learning_outcomes.topic: Recognise a pyramid, and identify its base and apex.
outcomes: Compute the surface area and volume of a pyramid.

learning_outcomes.topic: Graphical representation of data
outcomes: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question.
Read and interpret stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs.

learning_outcomes.topic: Measures of central tendency
outcomes: Compute and apply weighted average in different settings.
Apply computational thinking strategies to analyse real-life data, create appropriate graphical representations.

learning_outcomes.topic: Concept of probability and randomness
outcomes: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.

learning_outcomes.topic: The probability scale
outcomes: Understand the concept of randomness.
Describe the likelihood of an event using the probability scale.

learning_outcomes.topic: Empirical probability: analysing statistical data and performing experiments
outcomes: Estimate the empirical probability of the occurrence of an event by analysing statistical data.

learning_outcomes.topic: Theoretical probability: sample space and events
outcomes: Define theoretical probability of an event.
Apply the definition of theoretical probability to compute the probability of an event.

learning_outcomes.topic: Representing probability through tree diagrams and tables
outcomes: Compute probability of events with the help of tree diagrams and tables.
Use computational thinking strategies, such as pattern recognition and simulation.

activities.title: Constructing a Square using Baudhayana's Sulbasutras
activities.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
activities.details: Students will construct a square with a given side as described in the ancient Indian text, exploring historical geometric methods.

activities.title: Visualising Algebraic Identities using Geometrical Models
activities.details: Students will use geometric models to visualise and verify algebraic identities.

activities.title: Factorisation using Algebra Tiles
activities.details: Students will visualise the factorisation of quadratic expressions using algebra tiles and without them.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.details: Students will engage with the Tower of Hanoi puzzle to understand recursive rules and geometric progressions.

activities.title: Floor Plan Representation on a Grid
activities.details: Students will represent a floor plan using coordinates to specify locations and positions.

activities.title: Drawing, Measurement, and Paper Manipulation for 4-gons
activities.details: Students will engage in drawing, measurement, and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and quadrilaterals.

activities.title: Identifying Cultural Motifs Involving Circles
activities.details: Students will identify cultural motifs such as Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, and Sudarshan Chakra.

activities.title: Squaring a Rectangle using Sulbasutras Proof
activities.details: Students will explore the proof from Baudhayana's Sulbasutras regarding squaring a rectangle.

activities.title: Graphical Representation of Data Analysis
activities.details: Students will collect, organise, visualise, and interpret data to answer statistical investigative questions using stacked bar graphs.

activities.title: Empirical Probability Experiments
activities.details: Students will perform experiments to estimate empirical probability of events.

projects.title: Mathematics Laboratory Practical Activities
projects.details: Students will complete prescribed lab activities from the Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools and NCERT Laboratory Manual, covering geometry constructions, mensuration calculations, and statistical data analysis.

practicals.title: Geometry Constructions
practicals.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
practicals.details: Construction of a square with a given side using Baudhayana's Sulbasutras method.

practicals.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
practicals.details: Students will construct the square root spiral to visualise irrational numbers.

practicals.title: Circumcircle and Circumcentre Construction
practicals.details: Construction of the circumcircle and identification of the circumcentre for acute, obtuse, and right-angled triangles.

practicals.title: Data Collection and Graphical Analysis
practicals.details: Collecting real-life data to create stacked bar graphs and 100% stacked bar graphs for analysis.

dependent_topics: Representation of rational numbers on the number line

dependency_graph.topic: Density of rational numbers and its proof
prerequisites: Representation of rational numbers on the number line

dependency_graph.topic: Introduction to linear polynomials and applications

dependent_topics: Modelling linear growth and linear decay
Explicit or general rule of a sequence

dependency_graph.topic: Factorisation of algebraic expressions using identities
Introduction to linear equations in two variables through practical examples
dependent_topics: Solution of linear equation in two variables: graphical representation

dependency_graph.topic: Slope-intercept form of linear equation in two variables
prerequisites: Introduction to linear equations in two variables through practical examples

dependent_topics: Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
Axioms: Axioms of measurement and rules for geometric objects
Proofs of conditions of congruence of triangles

prerequisites: Important theorems related to parallelograms and their proof

dependency_graph.topic: Definitions related to a circle — centre, diameter, and radius
dependent_topics: Chords and the angles they subtend

prerequisites: Area of shapes: rectangles, parallelograms, and triangles

dependency_graph.topic: Surface areas and volumes of spheres (including hemispheres) and right circular cones

lesson_planning_intelligence.difficulty_level: Moderate to High (Proofs and Abstract Concepts)
lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density of abstract concepts regarding rationality, irrationality, and proofs.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Square root spiral construction required for visualizing irrationals.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on proof of irrationality and representation on number line.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in defining polynomials, degrees, and linear modeling.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Visualizing linear relationships using graphs is essential.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess ability to model growth/decay and identify patterns.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in recursive vs explicit rules and AP/GP applications.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Tower of Hanoi puzzle activity is critical for understanding recursion.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on deriving nth terms and visualizing AP/GP.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in geometric visualization of algebra.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Algebra tiles and geometrical models are mandatory for factorization understanding.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess visualisation of identities and simplification skills.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in graphical vs algebraic methods and consistency.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Graphical plotting of pairs of equations is a core requirement.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess both graphical and algebraic (substitution/elimination) solving methods.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Medium density, focused on spatial reasoning.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Floor plan representation activity is key for context.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on distance calculation and collinearity checks.

lesson_planning_intelligence.chapter_name: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Medium density, heavy on definitions and axioms.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Square construction via Sulbasutras is a foundational practical.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess understanding of postulates and historical context.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in theorems regarding parallel lines.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Proof by contradiction exercises are necessary.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on proving vertically opposite angles and linear pair theorem.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very high density in congruence conditions and converse propositions.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Identifying uses of triangle rigidity is a key conceptual step.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Heavy emphasis on proving congruence using SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very high density in parallelogram properties and midpoint theorem.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Paper manipulation for symmetry discovery is essential.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess proof of characterizations and central symmetry understanding.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Very high density in chord, arc, and angle relationships.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Circumcircle construction is a critical practical skill.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on proving properties of chords and cyclic quadrilaterals.

lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Squaring a rectangle proof activity is required for conceptual depth.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess derivation of circle area and application of Heron's formula.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: High density in formulas for various solids.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Identifying shapes in daily life is a prerequisite activity.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Focus on computing surface area and volume of composite solids.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Medium density, focused on graphical literacy.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Real-life data collection and graph creation is mandatory.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess ability to interpret stacked bar graphs.

lesson_planning_intelligence.concept_density: Medium density in theoretical vs empirical probability.
lesson_planning_intelligence.practical_requirement: Empirical experiments are required to bridge theory and practice.
lesson_planning_intelligence.assessment_requirement: Assess understanding of randomness and tree diagram usage.
